input.question: We are a student engineering team designing a vehicle for the FSAE competition. Attached is the FSAE rules document. What does rule EV.5.7 state exactly? Answer with only the text of the rule and no other words.

retrieved_context:
RULE: ev.5.7.2
            TITLE: the voltage being present at the connectors must directly control the voltage indicator using

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.5.7.2: The voltage being present at the connectors must directly control the Voltage Indicator using
EV.5.7.2 The voltage being present at the connectors must directly control the Voltage Indicator using 
hard wired electronics with no software control.

RULE: ev.5.7
            TITLE: voltage indicator

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.5.7: Voltage Indicator
EV.5.7 Voltage Indicator 
Each Accumulator Container must have a prominent indicator when High Voltage T.9.1.1 is 
present at the vehicle side of the AIRs

RULE: ev.5.7.1
            TITLE: the voltage indicator must always function, including when the accumulator container is

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.5.7.1: The Voltage Indicator must always function, including when the Accumulator Container is
EV.5.7.1 The Voltage Indicator must always function, including when the Accumulator Container is 
disconnected or removed

RULE: ev.5.7.4
            TITLE: the voltage indicator must:

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.5.7.4: The Voltage Indicator must:
EV.5.7.4 The Voltage Indicator must:  
a. Be located where it is clearly visible when connecting/disconnecting the Accumulator 
Tractive System connections 
b. Be labeled “High Voltage Present”

RULE: ev.5.7.3
            TITLE: the control signal which closes the airs must not control the voltage indicator

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.5.7.3: The control signal which closes the AIRs must not control the Voltage Indicator
EV.5.7.3 The control signal which closes the AIRs must not control the Voltage Indicator

final_answer: Voltage Indicator Each Accumulator Container must have a prominent indicator when High Voltage T.9.1.1 is present at the vehicle side of the AIRs
